meaning: A closed, reviewable HEIF/HEIC construction program. Entries become supported edges only after an independent Capsule, Adapter, correctness evidence and Capsule-local edge weight exist.

format_model.heif: ISO BMFF-derived image container graph for a single image, collection or sequence; not a pixel codec.
format_model.heic: An HEIF interoperability/profile family whose coded image items use HEVC; not merely a filename extension alias.
format_model.avif: A separate AV1 Image File Format profile that reuses the same container-layer research without being called HEIC.
format_model.computability_rule: Container-graph operators may be exact without decoding pixels. Pixel conversions require a declared codec, item-selection, color, alpha, bit-depth, orientation, grid and sequence policy.

standard_basis.name: ISO/IEC 23008-12:2025
standard_basis.role: HEIF image file format and codec brands

standard_basis.name: AV1 Image File Format
standard_basis.role: AVIF profile sharing the container layer

standard_basis.name: libheif
standard_basis.role: differential/reference codec backend and corpus oracle

hevc_pixel_codec.reason: Full HEVC conformance is a separate codec-scale state space; a partial native decoder may not masquerade as common HEIC support.
hevc_pixel_codec.replacement_gate: Differential corpus, conformance profile, color pipeline and quality evidence must pass before the native backend becomes default.

waves.exit_gate: All 20 crates copy out, build and test without EverythingX; malformed box, extent, reference-cycle, property-association and allocation-limit corpora pass.
Primary-image, alpha, HDR, 8/10/12-bit, chroma, range, color profile, orientation and grid policies are explicit; reference-backend differential tests and quality metrics pass.
Codec profile and timed-media semantics are never collapsed into an unqualified HEIF-to-PNG single-image claim.

performance_rule.production_requirement: Every implemented capability must be benchmarked by the controlled profile and receive a generated edge-weight.json in its Capsule root before support is claimed.
performance_rule.planner_use: Use concrete-input latency, peak-memory and output-size estimates as the edge vector; derived load_0_to_100 is only a same-profile equivalent-edge tie-breaker.
